title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony discusses his loyal servant, Berceuse, with Bartel, the chief of police. Anthony notes that Berceuse’s instinctual loyalty to the House of Paulovitch is remarkable. After Berceuse leads Anthony outside, Anthony encounters Virginia and Jimmy McGregor. Jimmy explains he traveled to join Anthony after being attacked with a knife following a failed attempt by Europeans to buy a manuscript.

Anthony reveals he has the real memoir manuscript, which he had hidden in a hotel safe after creating a fake one to deceive a publisher. He tasks Jimmy with delivering the package to Mr. Benson’s secret address in London for a reward of one thousand pounds. Anthony notes the manuscript is politically dull and lacks interesting anecdotes. Anthony then confesses his true identity to Virginia: he is the real Prince Nikolai of Hesse's Land. He tells her they will travel to Hesse's Land to become king and queen, jokingly noting the low life expectancy of monarchs there. After Jimmy departs to complete the delivery, Anthony and Virginia share a romantic moment.

The narrative shifts to Thursday morning at Chimney House, where Officer Johnson digs a symbolic "grave" during a somber gathering involving George Romax, Chief Batter, and Mr. Fitch. Following a formal procedure, George Romax examines the contents of a small bag. Elsewhere, the Duke of Cambridge urges his daughter, Benedict, to drive him to London immediately, expressing his desire to sell the chimney house to a consortium if the country does not buy it. Meanwhile, Bill Everly makes a phone call to Mott, and Mr. Fisk reflects on the eventful week.
